overview.description: Phase-one overview built from existing daily stats and replay data, focused on readable signals instead of new configuration.

trustedProxies.restartNotice: Changes take effect after service restart.

circuitBreaker.title: Circuit Breaker
circuitBreaker.hint: When a channel fails consecutively up to the threshold, circuit breaker trips. Cooldown grows exponentially up to the max cooldown

responseFilter.title: Response Keyword Filter
responseFilter.description: Intercept model output based on keywords. Supports blocking or replacing matched content.

action.label: Filter action
action.blockHint: Immediately stop the response and return an error when a keyword is detected.
action.replaceHint: Replace matched keywords with asterisks (*), the response continues normally.

keywords.label: Blocked keywords
keywords.placeholder: Type a keyword and press Enter to add
keywords.empty: No keywords yet. Add keywords to block.

errorMessage.label: Block error message
errorMessage.placeholder: Enter the error message returned when blocked
errorMessage.hint: The error message text returned to the client when a keyword is detected in block mode.

count.label: Max retries per channel
count.hint: Max key-level retries within a single candidate channel. 0 = no retry (try once, then switch channel on failure).

routeRetries.label: Route-level retries
routeRetries.hint: How many full rounds all candidate channels are traversed. 1 = traverse all channels once. Larger values retry exhausted channels across rounds.

ratelimitCooldown.label: Key cooldown (seconds)
ratelimitCooldown.hint: After a channel key returns an upstream error (e.g. 429 rate limit, 401/403 auth failure), the key is skipped for this cooldown period. Set to 0 to disable cooldown.

maxTotalAttempts.label: Max total attempts
maxTotalAttempts.hint: Caps real forwarded attempts across all candidate channels for one request. Skipped (cooldown) and circuit-broken attempts do not count. 0 means unlimited.

emptyOutput.label: Retry on empty output
emptyOutput.hint: Automatically retry when the upstream returns success but output is empty (non-streaming: CompletionTokens=0 and no content; streaming: no valid data chunks).

keySelectionStrategy.label: Key selection strategy
keySelectionStrategy.hint: Default strategy for choosing among multiple keys in a channel. 'cost' picks the lowest-cost key; 'availability' picks the highest-availability key (penalized on errors, recovers on success/time). Channels can override this individually.

autoStrategy.title: Auto Strategy
autoStrategy.hint: Configure the automatic channel selection strategy. Channels with insufficient samples are explored first, then selection is based on success rate.

minSamples.label: Min Samples
minSamples.hint: Minimum requests before using success rate for selection

timeWindow.label: Time Window (sec)
timeWindow.hint: Time window for calculating success rate

sampleThreshold.label: Sample Threshold
sampleThreshold.hint: Maximum number of samples to keep in sliding window

latencyWeight.label: Latency Weight (0-100)
latencyWeight.hint: Higher values prefer lower-latency channels more strongly; lower values lean more on success rate.

group.label: Default Target Group for Single-Group AI Routing
group.hint: Used by the compatibility single-group AI routing flow. Clicking “AI Route” on the route page generates the full routing table directly.

model.label: AI Model
model.placeholder: Enter the model used for route analysis

timeoutSeconds.label: AI Timeout (seconds)
timeoutSeconds.hint: Applies to a single AI route analysis request. Increase it if the model is slow or the model list is large.

parallelism.label: Batch Parallelism
parallelism.hint: Controls the maximum number of AI route buckets analyzed in parallel. Defaults to 3, allows higher values, and never exceeds the number of batches in the current task.

services.label: AI Service Pool (JSON)
services.placeholder: Enter a JSON array of AI services
services.hint: Leave empty or [] to keep using the single-service settings above. A JSON array enables multi-service pooling and failover.
services.invalid: AI service pool JSON must be an array

semanticCache.title: Semantic Cache
semanticCache.description: Configure the semantic cache runtime and its dedicated embedding service without leaving the current card layout.

ttl.label: Cache TTL (seconds)
ttl.hint: Cached entries expire after this duration.

threshold.label: Similarity Threshold (0-100)
threshold.hint: Higher values require a closer semantic match before a cached response is reused.

maxEntries.label: Max Entries
maxEntries.placeholder: Enter the maximum number of cached entries
maxEntries.hint: When the limit is reached, older cached entries are evicted first.

embedding.title: Embedding Service
embedding.description: These settings are used only for semantic-cache embedding requests and are not shared with AI Route.

baseUrl.label: Embedding Base URL
baseUrl.placeholder: Enter an OpenAI-compatible embedding base URL

timeoutSeconds.label: Embedding Timeout (seconds)
timeoutSeconds.hint: Applies only to semantic-cache embedding requests.

This removes three kinds of items from all route groups:
1. Models whose channel is disabled
2. Models a channel no longer provides after an update
3. Models left over after a channel was deleted
The groups themselves are kept. Continue?

normalize.title: Model Name Normalization
normalize.description: Rules to merge model name variants (e.g. dmxapi-kimi-k2.5, kimi-k2.5-cc) into a base name (kimi-k2.5) for aggregation and dedup. Used only for display, does not rewrite requests.

marketDedupeDefault.title: Dedup by default in Model Market
marketDedupeDefault.hint: When enabled, the Model Market page opens with normalization-based dedup turned on, so variant model names are merged into a single entry automatically. You can still toggle it off per session.

routerPrefixes.title: Router Prefixes
routerPrefixes.hint: Prefixes stripped from the start of a model name (e.g. dmxapi-). Case-insensitive. Enter with trailing dash.
routerPrefixes.empty: No custom router prefixes; built-in defaults are used

functionalSuffixes.title: Functional Suffixes
functionalSuffixes.hint: Suffixes stripped from the end of a model name (e.g. -cc, -latest). Case-insensitive, stacked suffixes are stripped repeatedly. Enter with leading dash.
functionalSuffixes.empty: No custom functional suffixes; built-in defaults are used

explicitMappings.title: Explicit Mappings
explicitMappings.hint: Point-to-point variant → canonical mappings (e.g. claude-3.5-sonnet → claude-3-5-sonnet). Primarily produced by offline AI analysis and imported; you can remove entries here. Highest priority in normalization.

workflow.title: Offline AI Workflow
workflow.description: Export channel model names (with variants) for offline AI/agent analysis, then import the produced normalization rules.
workflow.promptHint: Copy this prompt and paste it into your offline AI chat along with the exported channel model names JSON. The AI will produce normalization rules in the expected format.
workflow.prompt:
You are a model name normalization assistant. I will give you a JSON whose `channels` array contains objects with a `name` field (channel-declared model names, possibly with variants). Analyze these names, find naming variants of the same base model, and produce normalization rules.

Rules come in three categories:
1. router_prefixes: router/platform prefixes at the start of a model name that are unrelated to the underlying model, e.g. "dmxapi-", "agent-". Each entry must end with a hyphen.
2. functional_suffixes: functional suffixes at the end of a model name unrelated to the model itself, e.g. "-cc", "-fast", "-thinking". Each entry must start with a hyphen.
3. explicit_mappings: point-to-point variant→canonical mappings for cases prefix/suffix rules cannot cover, e.g. {"variant":"claude-3.5-sonnet","canonical":"claude-3-5-sonnet"}.

Principles:
- Only merge variants that truly belong to the same base model; avoid false merges (e.g. gpt-4o and gpt-4o-mini are different models and must not be merged).
- Provider path prefixes (e.g. moonshotai/, @cf/org/) are stripped automatically and need not be listed in router_prefixes.
- Prefer prefix/suffix rules; use explicit_mappings only when prefix/suffix cannot express the relationship.

Output ONLY a single JSON object, no explanation, in this format:
{
  "router_prefixes": ["..."],
  "functional_suffixes": ["..."],
  "explicit_mappings": [{"variant":"...","canonical":"..."}]
}

export.title: Export Channel Model Names
export.hint: Downloads all channel-declared model names (including variants like dmxapi-kimi-k2.5) as JSON, for offline AI variant analysis.
export.empty: No channel model names to export

import.title: Import Rules
import.hint: Upload a JSON file produced by offline AI analysis. Supports router_prefixes, functional_suffixes, and explicit_mappings. Imported rules merge into the editor (deduplicated); click Save to persist.
import.noRules: No valid rules found in file
import.applied: Rules merged into editor; click Save to persist

analysis.title: Coverage Analysis
analysis.description: Analyze channel model names against the current rules. Shows merged clusters (rule working) and suspected un-merged clusters (candidates to add as a prefix/suffix).
analysis.empty: No channel model names to analyze
analysis.mergedEmpty: No variants have been merged yet
analysis.disclaimer: Suspected clusters are inferred by prefix/suffix relationships and may include false positives (e.g. gpt-4o vs gpt-4o-mini). Review before adding.

autoGroup.confirmDescription:
This will scan all channel models and create groups for uncovered models based on normalization rules.
Existing groups will not be modified or deleted.
Continue?

table.confirmTitle: Generate the Full AI Routing Table?
table.confirmDescription:
This will send all models to the AI for analysis and generate the full routing table in batch.
Existing groups with the same name will have their route items synchronized, and stale route items will be removed; other groups will not be deleted.
Continue?

group.confirmTitle: Sync the Current Group with AI Routing?
group.confirmDescription:
This will send all models to the AI for analysis and synchronize the current group's route items with the result.
Stale route items will be removed, and existing matches will be updated to the latest priority and weight.
Continue?

summary.title: Model Market
summary.description: View model pricing, channel coverage, available keys, and runtime performance in one place while keeping the existing pricing workflow.

detail.noChannels: No channels cover this model yet

capabilities.title: Model Capabilities
capabilities.description: Declared endpoint support, conversation flag, and availability per model.
capabilities.note: * group items are narrowed at relay time for non-conversation endpoints. A model appearing here may still return 404 for an endpoint if its items do not actually support it.

ccSwitch.description: Generate a CC Switch v1 provider import link with a target app, route group, and Octopus API key.
ccSwitch.missingPublicApiBaseUrl: Please set Public API Base URL in system settings first.
ccSwitch.missingKey: Please configure at least one enabled API key for this channel.
ccSwitch.missingGroup: Create at least one route group first.
ccSwitch.missingAPIKey: Create and enable at least one Octopus API key first.

actions.checkAllFailed: All keys are unavailable, you can delete this channel
actions.deleteUnavailableTitle: Delete channel with all keys unavailable?
actions.deleteUnavailableDescription:
All {total} keys of this channel failed the check.
Deleting will permanently remove the channel, its keys and stats, and clear its route items from all route groups.
This action cannot be undone. Continue?

testModel.noModels: No models available to test for this channel
testModel.skipped: This channel is set to skip model availability tests (some upstream providers penalize low-byte probe requests). Testing is disabled.

groupManager.title: Channel Groups
groupManager.hint: Used for channel page management and display, and also as a grouping reference during AI route generation, but it does not directly rewrite existing routing or relay results.
groupManager.empty: There are no channel groups yet
groupManager.emptyGroup: There are no channels in this group

form.name: Channel Name
form.baseUrlHint: Enter the official or compatible service base address. OpenAI mode adds the protocol prefix; Custom only appends the concrete endpoint path.
form.baseUrlOpenAIHint: For example, if you enter https://api.example.com, the system expands it to https://api.example.com/v1, then requests /chat/completions, /responses, or /embeddings.
form.baseUrlOpenAIWarning: OpenAI mode is enabled. You do not need to add /v1 manually. For example, https://api.example.com becomes https://api.example.com/v1.
form.baseUrlCustomHint: For example, if you enter https://api.example.com/custom, the system does not add /v1 and only continues with /chat/completions, /responses, or /embeddings.
form.channelProxyPlaceholder: Optional: proxy for this channel (overrides global proxy)
form.paramOverridePlaceholder: Optional: JSON string to override request params
form.requestRewriteHint: Optional compatibility rewrite for stricter upstream request formats
form.modelRefreshSuccess: Fetched {count} models. Choose which ones to add

modelPicker.title: Choose models to add
modelPicker.description: Upstream returned {count} models. Select the models before adding them to this channel.

template.label: Built-in Templates
template.hint: One click to prefill common provider settings
template.pickerHint: Choose a built-in provider to prefill the form. If your upstream is not listed, skip this step and configure it manually.

descriptions.anthropic: Official Claude endpoint. Version paths are handled automatically.
descriptions.gemini: Official Google Gemini endpoint. Version paths are handled automatically.
descriptions.cloudflare: Cloudflare Workers AI endpoint. Set base_url to your account root (incl. /client/v4/accounts/{ACCOUNT_ID}).

test.hint: Batch tests all entered endpoint and key combinations. HTTP 200 and 429 are treated as pass.

form.matchRegexPlaceholder: Optional: regex pattern to match request model names

errors.databaseSchemaOutdatedRestart: Database schema is outdated; restart the service to apply the latest migrations

advanced.title: Site Channel Advanced Settings
advanced.description: Configure parameter overrides for projected channels under group {group}.
advanced.empty: No projected channels in this group
advanced.paramOverridePlaceholder: Leave empty to clear. Must be a valid JSON object.
advanced.invalidParamOverride: Parameter override must be a valid JSON object

proxyPool.name: Proxy
proxyPool.disabledSelected: The selected proxy is currently disabled.

dialog.title: Proxy Pool
dialog.description: Manage proxy configurations for sites and channels.
dialog.deleteReferenced: Cannot delete — still referenced by other entities.
dialog.formDescription: Fill in proxy details below and save.
dialog.name: Name

modelFilter.dedupeHint: Merge naming variants of the same base model (e.g. kimi-k2.5, moonshotai/kimi-k2.5, dmxapi-kimi-k2.5-cc).
modelFilter.filterSummary: {count, plural, =0 {No filters} one {# filter} other {# filters}} active
